student_name: Eliane Portela Nogueira Lima de Brito
hub_subtitle: Business English for a doctor rebuilding her professional voice

lesson.menu_title: Who Is Eliane? — Diagnostic & Rebuilding Your Professional Voice
lesson.menu_desc: Describing who you are and what you do, in English again -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 1 -- Diagnostic Session & Rebuilding Your Professional Voice
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 1 -- Who Is Eliane?

listenings.text: Good evening, everyone, and welcome to the opening session of our executive education program. My name is Mark, and I will be your program coordinator this week. Over the next few days, you will meet leaders from more than twenty countries, from medicine, technology, and finance. Tonight is simple: we just want you to relax and connect. In a few minutes, I will ask each of you to introduce yourself in one minute. Please tell us your name, where you are from, what you do, and one project you are working on right now. Do not worry about perfect grammar. We care about your story, not your accent. So take a breath, and let us begin.

Hi Eliane, it's Dr. Sofia Reyes calling from New York. It has been a long time since your months here at the medical center, and I was thinking about you today. I heard that you have been building something new in reproductive medicine back in Brazil, and I would love to hear more. We are organizing a small case discussion next month, and I immediately thought of your work with fertility patients. I know your English is a little rusty, but honestly, mine was too when I started, and it comes back faster than you think. Give me a call when you have a moment. It would be wonderful to reconnect. Take care, and talk soon.
